# RustyNES v2.0.8 — "Harbor" ("Harborlight")

**The iOS release candidate.** The eighth release of the v2.0.x "Harbor" mobile-finalization train and the **final release of the iOS finalization window (v2.0.5 → v2.0.8)**. It runs on the byte-identical **v2.0.0 "Timebase"** core — **AccuracyCoin 141/141 (100.00%)**, nestest 0-diff, the `#![no_std]` chip stack untouched — so **no accuracy, save-state, or determinism number moves**. This is a host / iOS-only cut that stages the App Store scaffolding a maintainer needs for the v2.1.0 launch.

No store submission happens here (that is the future v2.1.0 joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch); v2.0.8 brings the iOS app to the point a maintainer can drive the on-device closeout and the store submission.

---

## The core is unchanged (141/141)

Nothing in the emulation core changed since v2.0.3. AccuracyCoin holds **141/141 (100.00%, RAM-authoritative)**, nestest is 0-diff.

## App Store Connect listing metadata (version-controlled, files only)

`fastlane/metadata/ios/{en-US,es-ES}/` stages the store listing as tracked source: `name`, `subtitle`, `promotional_text`, `keywords`, `description`, `release_notes`, `support_url`, `marketing_url`, plus a top-level `copyright.txt`. It mirrors the Android `fastlane/metadata/android/` tree, **namespaced under `ios/`** so `fastlane deliver` (iOS) and `fastlane supply` (Android) never collide. Copy is accurate to the app (a cycle-accurate NES emulator, bring-your-own-ROMs, open source, English + Spanish) and flags the release-candidate / TestFlight status. **No upload** — a maintainer pushes it via the lane below at v2.1.0.

## Dormant App Store `release` lane

`fastlane/Fastfile` gains a `release` lane that stages the signed build + the listing metadata via `upload_to_app_store` with **`submit_for_review: false`** and **`automatic_release: false`** — it never auto-submits. It is intentionally **not** wired into `.github/workflows/ios.yml`; the interim iOS channel stays **TestFlight** (the `beta` lane) until v2.1.0, when a maintainer runs `fastlane ios release` with the signing secrets provisioned and completes submission from App Store Connect. Screenshots are captured on-device separately (`skip_screenshots: true`).

## App-Review §4.7 self-audit

A self-audit against Apple's §4.7 (emulators) is recorded in `docs/ios-v2.0.8-readiness.md`: **no bundled or downloadable ROMs** (user-sourced via the Files picker only), **no in-app ROM links**, **no Nintendo branding / art**, an in-app **ownership notice** (onboarding), a **searchable library** (the ROM library), a **4+ age rating** (the app is a tool; user content is out of scope), and the NES-ROMs-carry-no-encryption / post-*Nintendo v. Yuzu* posture. RustyNES is a pure interpreter, so the no-JIT rule is a non-issue.

## Verification (host)

- **Core unchanged** — AccuracyCoin **141/141**, nestest 0-diff (not re-run; chip stack untouched).
- `cargo check --workspace` (version-only lock cascade) + `fmt` + `clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings` (no issues) + `RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings cargo doc` + the `no_std` cross-compile + both wasm32 clippy gates — all green.
- markdownlint (CI-pinned) clean; the metadata is plain text within App Store field limits; the `Fastfile` lane is dormant (not CI-invoked).
- The Swift/Xcode build + on-device validation remain the documented macOS / TestFlight carryover.

## Maintainer / on-device closeout (v2.0.9 / v2.1.0)

- Capture **App Store screenshots + the app preview** on device (iPhone + iPad; cannot be generated on the Linux host).
- Provision the App Store Connect API key + fastlane match, archive with **Xcode 26 / the iOS 26 SDK**, and run `fastlane ios beta` (TestFlight) — then `fastlane ios release` at v2.1.0 to stage the App Store listing.
- Complete the App-Review submission from App Store Connect; set the age rating + privacy answers.
- The full v1.9.9 on-device TestFlight checklist.

## What's next

- **v2.0.9** — joint on-device readiness verification for **both** apps (Android + iOS): the batched on-device pass, the `foss`/`play` Android flavors, live monetization, and the iOS crash-capture + Xcode-26 archive.
- **v2.1.0** — the **joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch** (flip every gated flag; submit both apps together).

Full detail: `CHANGELOG.md` `[2.0.8]` · `docs/STATUS.md` (single source of truth) · `docs/ios-v2.0.8-readiness.md` · `to-dos/plans/v2.0.5-v2.0.8-ios-finalization-plan.md`.
